name: Trigger.dev
what_it_does: An open-source platform for building and deploying fully-managed AI agents and long-running workflows in TypeScript, with durable task execution, automatic retries, queues, human-in-the-loop pauses, and full observability.
use_cases: Building AI agents that need to run for minutes or hours without serverless function timeouts
Durable multi-step AI workflows with automatic retry on failure and human approval gates
Scheduled AI tasks (cron) running up to yearly intervals
Batch AI processing pipelines with queue management and concurrency control
AI workflows requiring custom system packages, browser automation, or Python script execution
not_for: Simple webhook receivers or short-lived API handlers (overkill for sub-second tasks)
Teams needing a no-code workflow builder (Trigger.dev is code-first TypeScript)
Non-TypeScript/JavaScript projects without willingness to add a Node.js component
best_when: You are building AI agents or workflows in TypeScript that need to run longer than serverless limits allow, with production-grade reliability, retries, and observability built in.
avoid_when: Your workflows are short-lived and simple enough for standard serverless functions, or your team works exclusively in non-TypeScript languages.

auth.notes: Project-scoped API keys for triggering tasks and accessing the management API. React hooks provide frontend integration patterns.

paid_tiers: Cloud paid tiers for higher volume and team features
Self-hosted via Docker/Kubernetes (free, requires own infrastructure)
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed. Cloud version has free tier. Self-hosting is fully supported.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Mature platform with comprehensive observability; error traces and logs are core product features.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Durable execution model with idempotency keys and retry semantics is a core platform feature.
known_agent_gotchas: Not an MCP server itself - it is an agent/workflow execution platform that agents are built on top of
TypeScript/JavaScript only SDK - not suitable for Python or other language agent stacks without a wrapper
Human-in-the-loop workflows require the frontend React hooks integration for approval UI
Task runs are limited to 10 tags for filtering - may be limiting for complex multi-tenant setups
